title: Hormuz blockade economic impact
summary: Jermy estimates 18-month GDP recovery lag for strait closure affecting diesel supply chains and fertilizer pricing across importers.

title: Islamabad process stall signals
summary: Marandi and Parsi diverge on whether the current diplomatic pause reflects genuine back-channel progress or exhaustion of available formats.

title: Davis extension-game thesis
summary: Davis argues ceasefire proposals function as extension games buying time for force repositioning rather than genuine diplomatic openings.

title: Pape escalation trap framework
summary: Pape maps commitment ratchet on demands with staged branches including nuclear stockpile logic and ground-force scenarios.

title: Parsi Lebanon-vs-nuclear scope conflict
summary: Parsi contends Beltway framing conflates Lebanon negotiations with nuclear scope, masking the real US objective hierarchy.

title: Mercouris institutional read on Hormuz
summary: Mercouris frames the blockade as institutional test for EU-level response capacity, highlighting delegation gaps between member states on energy policy.

title: MCP adapter portability shipped
summary: Wave 4b of the MCP adapter shipped with full portability layer, including plugin discovery and hot-reload support across companion-self instances.

title: Runtime bundle export latency
summary: Export runtime bundle takes 1.1s average; dominant cost is fork-export.json assembly at 800ms. Profile shows redundant SHA256 recomputation.

title: Integrity check stale-export pattern
summary: Stale derived exports are the most common integrity failure mode. Pattern: edit Record surface, forget to regenerate derived exports before dream.

title: Barnes domestic liability analysis
summary: Barnes traces the executive chain on Hormuz operations through War Powers constraints and coalition-sell dynamics in the Senate.

title: Mearsheimer structural realism on Israel
summary: Mearsheimer applies offensive realism framework to Israel security dilemma, arguing great-power geometry constrains available alliance options.

title: Persian imperial continuity thesis
summary: HN chapter hn-i-v1-04 traces legitimacy transfer mechanisms from Achaemenid to Safavid periods, identifying four recurrent institutional patterns.

title: Roman institutional adaptation under stress
summary: HN chapter maps how Roman administrative structures survived political crisis through delegation and procedural absorption, paralleling modern NATO expansion strain.

title: Sachs deinstitutionalization thesis
summary: Sachs argues Congress has vacated war-and-peace decisions to personalized executive action, creating a structural gap in democratic accountability on Hormuz.

title: Skill-card schema gap
summary: build_skill_cards.py imports SCHEMA_PATH but never validates output cards against it. Malformed cards can be written to disk without error.
